## Prison Admissions
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Prison Admission Trends

Sentence about change in admissions from 2010 to 2020.



Admission Types

Prison admissions are shaped by two primary groups: new court commitments, or individuals newly incarcerated for a criminal offense, and parole returns and revocations, those who return to prison for failing to meet the conditions of their parole.

In Louisiana in 2020, new court commitments accounted for 76% of prison admissions, while parole returns and revocations accounted for 24%.


``` ::: :::

## Parole Eligibility
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

How is Parole Eligibility Determined?

Each crime in Louisiana appears to have a statutory maximum. For example, theft of something with a value of $25,000 or over will result in imprisonment of not more than twenty years or a fine of not more than fifty thousand dollars, or both. Any sentence to imprisonment must be “determinate,” in other words, judges sentence individuals to a maximum sentence rather than a range with minimum and maximum limits. Eligibility for parole is determined by type of crime and by criminal history. Unusually, prisoners who have been convicted of non-violent, non-sexual offenses can also age out of the Louisiana prison system even if they are ineligible for parole. If sentenced to a term of over 30 years, a prisoner who has served at least 20 of those years and has reached the age of 45 is eligible for parole consideration. If sentenced to any term without the benefit of parole, a prisoner who has reached the age of 60 and has completed various institutional requirements may be eligible for parole.


``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Prison Population Currently Eligible for Parole

Show breakdown of types:
1) New court commitments/1-24.99 years
2) New court commitments/<1 & >= 25 years
3) Parole returns/1-24.99 years
4) Parole returns/<1 & >= 25 years


``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Focusing on New Court Commitments with
Sentences of 1 to 24.99 Years

For a nuanced analysis, this section narrows the focus to individuals serving sentences of 1 to 24.99 years for new court commitments.

In 2020, there were 2,218 people who were in prison and currently eligible for parole. This group made up 19% of the prison population in prison for new court commitments with sentences between 1 to 24.99 years.


``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Demographics

``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .tab2} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, among the prison population currently eligible for parole, Unknown individuals constituted the majority, representing 100 percent.


``` ::: ::: ### Gender ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, among the prison population currently eligible for parole, males constituted the majority, representing 94 percent.


``` ::: ::: ### Age ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, among the prison population currently eligible for parole, people between the ages of 25-34 years constituted the majority, representing 34 percent.


``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Criminal Offenses

In 2020, among the prison population currently eligible for parole, people with drugs offenses constituted the majority, representing 27 percent.


``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Sentence Lengths

In 2020, among the prison population currently eligible for parole, people with sentences between 10 to 24.9 years years constituted the majority, representing 42 percent.



``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Missing Data

This section will have information about missing parole eligibility data for the state once new NCRP data is released.

``` ::: :::

## Prison Releases
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Prison Release Trends

Sentence about change in releases from 2010 to 2020.



This chart shows an annual breakdown from 2010 to 2020 on the proportion of the parole-eligible population that was released each year compared to those who remained incarcerated despite their parole eligibility.



``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Prison Release Types

Sentence about conditional and unconditional releases.




``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Timing of Release

This chart illustrates the timing of releases in relation to parole eligibility year by release type in 2020, comparing conditional and unconditional releases. It shows the proportion of individuals released before their parole eligibility year, during the year they were eligible, within one to five years after eligibility, more than five years after, and those with missing parole eligibility data.



This graphic depicts the distribution of release timings for different types of offenses in 2020. It shows the percentages of individuals released at various intervals relative to their parole eligibility year, ranging from those released before their parole eligibility year to those who remained incarcerated more than five years after becoming eligible for parole.



``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
